H
⌂Dashboard◫Stack▲Deploy>Commands?Help
KCommand Palette Ctrl+K

🚀 Quick Start

New to Hylono? Follow these 3 steps to get the stack running. Each step builds on the last.

📋 Before you start — Prerequisites
  • • Docker Desktop must be running on your machine
  • • You need at least 8 GB RAM available for the infrastructure layer
  • • You must be in the project root directory when running commands
  • • On Windows, run commands in Git Bash or WSL (not PowerShell)
1
🔑

Generate your secrets

This creates a .env file with unique random passwords for every service. You only do this once.

bash scripts/generate-secrets.sh

✅ Safe to run — it skips if .env already exists.

2
🏗

Start the infrastructure layer

This boots the 5 core services: PostgreSQL (database), Redis (cache), MinIO (file storage), MongoDB (used by Novu), and Uptime Kuma (monitoring).

bash scripts/setup.sh infrastructure

⏱ Takes ~30 seconds the first time (downloading Docker images).

3
🚀

Start Phase 1A services

Launches the first set of application services: Medusa (commerce), Lago (billing), Snipe-IT (assets), Cal.com (scheduling), Twenty CRM, Documenso (e-signatures), Zitadel (auth), Novu (notifications), n8n (automation).

bash scripts/setup.sh 1a

⏱ Takes 1–3 minutes. Leihs requires manual build (see Help page).

✅ After setup — service URLs

Once running, each service is accessible at its port on your machine:

Uptime Kuma (monitoring)http://localhost:3001
MinIO console (files)http://localhost:9001
n8n (automation)http://localhost:5678
Medusa (commerce)http://localhost:8100
Lago (billing)http://localhost:8102
Snipe-IT (assets)http://localhost:8104
Twenty CRMhttp://localhost:8107
Zitadel (auth)http://localhost:8109
